notable_pick_trades: Traded 5th-round pick to BAL for EDGE Odafe Oweh (Oct 2025); traded 7th-round pick to TEN for S Elijah Molden (Aug 2024). May receive compensatory picks (TBD spring 2026).
roster_context: The Chargers went 11-6 in 2025 under Jim Harbaugh but lost in the Wild Card to New England. Injuries to both OTs Rashawn Slater and Joe Alt devastated the offense — LA posted the NFL's worst pass-blocking grade (47.6 PFF) and allowed pressure on 37%+ of dropbacks. The interior OL was also poor, with C Bradley Bozeman retiring, G Mekhi Becton released, and G Zion Johnson hitting free agency. New OC Mike McDaniel brings an outside-zone scheme requiring athletic, agile linemen. Defensively, DC Jesse Minter left to become Ravens HC and was replaced by Chris O'Leary. Both EDGE Khalil Mack and Odafe Oweh are UFAs (not franchise tagged), creating a potential pass-rush crisis opposite Pro Bowler Tuli Tuipulotu. TE Will Dissly was also released, leaving only Oronde Gadsden under contract. The team has ~$98M in cap space and significant roster holes to fill via both free agency and the draft.

needs.explanation: The most pressing need on the entire roster. C Bradley Bozeman retired after being one of PFF's lowest-graded centers. RG Mekhi Becton was released on March 4, saving $9.7M but leaving a void. LG Zion Johnson is an UFA and may be too expensive to retain. The Chargers need to find up to three new interior starters between LT Rashawn Slater and RT Joe Alt. New OC Mike McDaniel's outside-zone scheme demands quick, athletic linemen — the opposite of what Becton provided. GM Joe Hortiz explicitly stated: 'Obviously, we need to add to the offensive line. We're frankly thin in terms of numbers there right now.'
needs.current_starter: Zion Johnson (LG, UFA), Bradley Bozeman (C, retired), Mekhi Becton (RG, released)
needs.depth: Andre James (UFA), Trevor Penning (UFA), Bobby Hart (UFA) — virtually no IOL under contract
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 (Penn State G Olaivavega Ioane or Alabama OL Kadyn Proctor are popular mocks at #22)

needs.explanation: Both Khalil Mack (35, UFA) and Odafe Oweh (UFA, projected $25M AAV) are set to hit free agency with no franchise tag applied. Losing both would be catastrophic. Pro Bowler Tuli Tuipulotu (13.0 sacks) is the anchor but needs a complementary rusher to limit double-teams. Justin Eboigbe (6 sacks as a DT/EDGE tweener) and rookie Kyle Kennard provide some depth but neither is a proven starter opposite Tuipulotu. The team has indicated willingness to replace via the draft if the price is too high in FA, and this EDGE class is deep.
needs.current_starter: Tuli Tuipulotu (under contract, final year of rookie deal), Khalil Mack (UFA), Odafe Oweh (UFA)
needs.depth: Justin Eboigbe (under contract), Kyle Kennard (under contract, 2025 draft pick), Bud Dupree (status unclear)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 if both Mack and Oweh depart; Round 3+ if one is re-signed

needs.explanation: The young core of Cam Hart and Tarheeb Still is solidified for the future, and Donte Jackson is under contract after a strong 2025 with multiple interceptions. However, Benjamin St-Juste (UFA) and Ja'Sir Taylor (status TBD) could depart, thinning depth. A third cornerback to develop alongside Hart and Still would be prudent, especially with Donte Jackson entering his 30s.
needs.current_starter: Tarheeb Still, Cam Hart, Donte Jackson (all under contract)
needs.depth: Benjamin St-Juste (UFA), Deane Leonard (UFA), Nikko Reed (under contract)

needs.explanation: Rashawn Slater and Joe Alt are expected to return healthy in 2026 after both suffered season-ending injuries in 2025. When healthy, this is one of the NFL's best tackle duos. However, the catastrophic 2025 injuries exposed a total lack of swing tackle depth — Jamaree Salyer (UFA), Trey Pipkins (UFA), Bobby Hart (UFA), and Trevor Penning (UFA) are all departing. The Chargers need a developmental swing tackle to insure against future injuries.
needs.current_starter: Rashawn Slater (LT, under contract), Joe Alt (RT, under contract)
needs.depth: Austin Deculus (RFA), everyone else is a UFA — extremely thin after starters

needs.explanation: Tony Jefferson (34, UFA) started alongside Derwin James and had a productive season (3 INTs) but is a free agent and aging. Derwin James is entering the final year of his contract. Elijah Molden is under contract and versatile, and R.J. Mickens (2025 6th-round pick) showed flashes with two INTs. The team could use a long-term starter alongside James, particularly if Jefferson departs. There are concerns about continuity with the DC transition from Minter to O'Leary.
needs.current_starter: Derwin James Jr. (under contract, final year), Tony Jefferson (UFA)
needs.depth: Elijah Molden (under contract), R.J. Mickens (under contract), Kendall Williamson (UFA)

needs.explanation: Will Dissly was released on March 4 and Tyler Conklin is a UFA after a disappointing season (7 catches, 101 yards). Oronde Gadsden II is the only TE under contract — he had a strong rookie year (664 yards, 3 TDs) as a receiving threat but needs help, particularly with in-line blocking. Under Mike McDaniel, athletic TEs who can create mismatches are coveted. Multiple mocks have the Chargers targeting Oregon TE Kenyon Sadiq at #22. A blocking Y-TE is also needed to complement Gadsden's receiving ability.
needs.depth: Tucker Fisk (RFA) is the only other option; Conklin UFA, Dissly released
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 for an elite TE like Kenyon Sadiq; Round 4-6 for a blocking TE

needs.explanation: Ladd McConkey is the clear WR1. Keenan Allen (34) is unsigned but expected to return on a low-cost deal. WR depth is adequate for 2026 but lacks a true WR2 behind McConkey at the starter level. Not a top-tier draft need — more of a value addition in rounds 3-5 if the right prospect is available.

Teair Tart was re-signed to a multi-year deal ($20M guaranteed) and is the anchor, but the interior DL needs additional help particularly against the run. Da'Shawn Hand (UFA) and Otito Ogbonnia (UFA) are both departing. The Chargers lacked beef on the interior when Tart wasn't on the field — PFF noted they struggled against 'better run teams.' Adding a run-stuffing 1-tech or 3-tech to pair with Tart is important, especially with a new DC in Chris O'Leary.
needs.current_starter: Teair Tart (under contract), Justin Eboigbe (under contract, versatile DL/EDGE)
needs.depth: Scott Matlock (under contract), Da'Shawn Hand (UFA), Otito Ogbonnia (UFA)

needs.explanation: Omarion Hampton (2025 1st-round pick) is the centerpiece back and showed flashes despite injuries. Kimani Vidal (under contract) ran for 643 yards and 3 TDs as a fill-in and provides solid depth. Najee Harris (UFA) missed most of 2025 with an Achilles injury and likely won't return. The position is reasonably set but adding a pass-catching back for McDaniel's scheme could elevate the offense.
needs.depth: Kimani Vidal (under contract), Jaret Patterson (ERFA); Najee Harris (UFA), Hassan Haskins (UFA)

free_agency_impact: Signed Teair Tart (IDL) from Los Angeles Chargers (re-sign/extension) — $10.0M AAV
Signed Khalil Mack (EDGE) from Los Angeles Chargers (re-sign) — $18.0M AAV
Signed Tyler Biadasz (IOL) from Washington Commanders — $10.0M AAV
Signed Charlie Kolar (TE) from Baltimore Ravens — $8.1M AAV
Signed Alec Ingold (RB) from Miami Dolphins — $3.8M AAV
Signed Cole Strange (IOL) from Miami Dolphins — $6.5M AAV
Signed Trevor Penning (IOL) from Los Angeles Chargers (re-sign) — $4.5M AAV
Signed Kimani Vidal (RB) from Los Angeles Chargers (ERFA tender) — $0.9M AAV
Signed Jaret Patterson (RB) from Los Angeles Chargers (ERFA tender) — $0.9M AAV
scheme_fit_notes: The Chargers run a 3-4 defensive base under new DC Chris O'Leary (Minter disciple). EDGE prospects need to be versatile stand-up rushers who can also drop in coverage. IDL players must be stout against the run to free up LBs. On offense, new OC Mike McDaniel brings an outside-zone scheme requiring athletic, quick-footed IOL who can move laterally — a massive departure from Greg Roman's gap-power scheme. This makes heavy, slow linemen (like Becton) poor fits and elevates the value of guards/centers with lateral agility and zone-blocking experience. TEs who can create mismatches as receivers and block on the move are premium in McDaniel's system.
